title: Paracetamol acetaminophen
text: Paracetamol, also called acetaminophen, is used for fever and mild to moderate pain. Avoid exceeding recommended daily dose because overdose can cause severe liver injury. Use caution with liver disease, heavy alcohol use, or when taking other medicines containing acetaminophen. Ask a clinician for child dosing.

title: Ibuprofen
text: Ibuprofen is a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug used for pain, fever, and inflammation. It can irritate the stomach and may increase bleeding, kidney, blood pressure, or heart risks in some people. Avoid in late pregnancy unless prescribed. Use caution with ulcers, kidney disease, blood thinners, and heart disease.

title: Amoxicillin
text: Amoxicillin is a penicillin antibiotic used for selected bacterial infections. It does not treat viral infections. Possible side effects include diarrhea, nausea, rash, and allergic reactions. Seek urgent help for breathing difficulty, facial swelling, or severe rash after taking it.
